# better-openclaw — Full Documentation

> Comprehensive AI-friendly reference for the better-openclaw stack generator.
> 75 services, 10 skill packs, 9 presets, 19 categories.

---

## 1. Project Overview

better-openclaw is a TypeScript monorepo that generates production-ready Docker Compose stacks pre-wired with OpenClaw agent skills. It provides:

- **CLI** (create-better-openclaw): Interactive wizard + non-interactive mode
- **REST API** (@better-openclaw/api): Hono-based API with OpenAPI 3.1 spec
- **Web UI** (@better-openclaw/web): Next.js 15 visual stack builder
- **Core** (@better-openclaw/core): Schemas, registry, resolver, composer, generators

### Architecture Pipeline

```
User Input → Resolve → Partition → Compose → Validate → Generate
```

1. **Resolve**: Expands services, adds dependencies (requires/recommends), resolves skill packs, adds proxy and monitoring services, detects conflicts
2. **Partition** (bare-metal only): Splits services into native vs Docker groups
3. **Compose**: Generates docker-compose.yml, .env, reverse proxy configs, skill files
4. **Validate**: Checks port conflicts, resource limits, platform compatibility
5. **Generate**: Outputs files as JSON, ZIP, or directory

---

## 2. API Reference

Base: `/v1`

### GET /v1/health
Returns API status, version, uptime, service count, memory usage.

### GET /v1/services
Query params: `category` (string), `maturity` (stable|beta|experimental)
Returns: `{ services: ServiceDefinition[], categories: CategoryInfo[], total: number }`

### GET /v1/skills
Query params: `services` (comma-separated IDs to filter compatible packs)
Returns: `{ skillPacks: SkillPack[], total: number, filteredBy?: string[] }`

### GET /v1/presets
Returns: `{ presets: Preset[], total: number }`

### POST /v1/validate
Body (ValidateRequestSchema):
```json
{
  "services": ["redis", "postgresql"],
  "skillPacks": [],
  "proxy": "none",
  "domain": "example.com",
  "gpu": false,
  "platform": "linux/amd64"
}
```
Returns: `{ valid: boolean, resolvedServices: string[], addedDependencies, warnings, conflicts, estimatedMemoryMB }`

### POST /v1/generate
Body (GenerationInputSchema):
```json
{
  "projectName": "my-stack",
  "services": ["ollama", "qdrant", "n8n"],
  "skillPacks": ["ollama-local-llm"],
  "proxy": "caddy",
  "domain": "example.com",
  "gpu": false,
  "platform": "linux/amd64",
  "deployment": "local",
  "deploymentType": "docker",
  "generateSecrets": true,
  "openclawVersion": "latest",
  "monitoring": false
}
```
Query params: `format` (json|zip|complete)
Headers: `Accept: application/zip` for ZIP output
Returns: `{ files: Record<string, string>, metadata: GenerationMetadata }`
Or with `?format=complete`: `{ formatVersion: "1", input, files, metadata }`
Or with `?format=zip`: Binary ZIP file

### GET /v1/openapi.json
Auto-generated OpenAPI 3.1 specification (via @hono/zod-openapi)

### GET /v1/docs
Interactive Swagger UI for the API

### Rate Limiting
- Global: 30 req/min anonymous, 300 req/min with X-API-Key header
- Generate endpoint: 5 req/min anonymous, 10 req/min with key
- Headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset
- Optional Redis backend (set REDIS_URL env var) for distributed rate limiting

---

## 3. Service Catalog (75 services)

### AI Coding Agents
- claude-code: Claude Code AI coding agent (anthropic/claude-code:latest, port 8080)
- codex: OpenAI Codex CLI agent (openai/codex:latest, port 8081)
- gemini-cli: Google Gemini CLI agent (google/gemini-cli:latest, port 8082)
- opencode: OpenCode AI coding agent (opencode/opencode:latest, port 8083)

### AI Platforms & Chat UIs
- anything-llm: AnythingLLM knowledge base + chat (mintplexlabs/anythingllm, port 3001)
- dify: Dify AI workflow platform (langgenius/dify, port 3100)
- flowise: Flowise low-code AI builder (flowiseai/flowise, port 3200)
- kimi: Kimi AI assistant interface (kimi-ai/kimi, port 3300)
- librechat: LibreChat multi-model AI chat (ghcr.io/danny-avila/librechat, port 3080)
- litellm: LiteLLM proxy for LLM APIs (ghcr.io/berriai/litellm, port 4000)
- open-webui: Open WebUI for Ollama (ghcr.io/open-webui/open-webui, port 3000)

### AI / Local Models
- ollama: Ollama local LLM server (ollama/ollama, port 11434)
- stable-diffusion: Stable Diffusion WebUI (ghcr.io/automatic1111/stable-diffusion-webui, port 7860)
- whisper: OpenAI Whisper speech-to-text (onerahmet/openai-whisper-asr-webservice, port 9000)

### Automation & Workflows
- cal-com: Cal.com scheduling platform (calcom/cal.com, port 3005→3000)
- n8n: n8n workflow automation (n8nio/n8n, port 5678)
- temporal: Temporal workflow orchestration (temporalio/auto-setup, port 7233)
- xyops: xyOps job scheduling, workflow automation, and monitoring (ghcr.io/pixlcore/xyops, port 5522/5523)

### Vector Databases
- chromadb: ChromaDB vector database (chromadb/chroma, port 8000)
- qdrant: Qdrant vector search engine (qdrant/qdrant, port 6333/6334)
- weaviate: Weaviate vector database (cr.weaviate.io/semitechnologies/weaviate, port 8082)

### Media & Video
- comfyui: ComfyUI node-based AI image generation (comfyui/comfyui, port 8188)
- ffmpeg: FFmpeg media processing (linuxserver/ffmpeg, no exposed ports)
- motion-canvas: Motion Canvas animation (motioncanvas/motion-canvas, port 9001)
- remotion: Remotion video rendering (remotion-dev/remotion, port 8011)

### Social Media
- mixpost: Mixpost social media management (inovector/mixpost, port 9000)
- postiz: Postiz social media scheduler (ghcr.io/gitroomhq/postiz-app, port 5000)
- usesend: UseSend email marketing (usesend/usesend, port 3000)

### Analytics
- matomo: Matomo web analytics (matomo, port 8080)
- openpanel: OpenPanel web analytics (openpanel/openpanel, port 3000)
- umami: Umami web analytics (ghcr.io/umami-software/umami, port 3000)

### Knowledge & Documents
- docsgpt: DocsGPT document AI assistant (arc53/docsgpt, port 5173)
- nocodb: NocoDB no-code database (nocodb/nocodb, port 8080)
- outline: Outline wiki and knowledge base (outlinewiki/outline, port 3000)
- paperless-ngx: Paperless-ngx document management (ghcr.io/paperless-ngx/paperless-ngx, port 8000)

### Object Storage
- minio: MinIO S3-compatible storage (minio/minio, port 9000/9001)

### Database & Caching
- convex: Convex reactive database (convex-dev/convex, port 3210)
- convex-dashboard: Convex Dashboard (convex-dev/convex-dashboard, port 6791)
- neo4j: Neo4j graph database (neo4j:5-community, port 7474/7687)
- postgresql: PostgreSQL relational database (postgres:16-alpine, port 5432)
- redis: Redis in-memory store (redis:7-alpine, port 6379)
- valkey: Valkey Redis-compatible store (valkey/valkey:8-alpine, port 6379)

### Developer Tools
- appflowy: AppFlowy project management (appflowy-cloud/appflowy-cloud, port 9001)
- beszel: Beszel server monitoring (henrygd/beszel, port 8090)
- code-server: Code Server VS Code in browser (linuxserver/code-server, port 8443)
- coolify: Coolify self-hosted PaaS (coollabsio/coolify, port 8000)
- dokploy: Dokploy deployment platform (dokploy/dokploy, port 3000)
- dozzle: Dozzle Docker log viewer (amir20/dozzle, port 8080)
- gitea: Gitea self-hosted Git service (gitea/gitea, port 3000/22)
- mission-control: Mission Control dashboard (mission-control/mission-control, port 3100)
- portainer: Portainer Docker management (portainer/portainer-ce, port 9443)
- watchtower: Watchtower Docker auto-updater (containrrr/watchtower, no exposed ports)

### Reverse Proxy
- caddy: Caddy reverse proxy with auto-SSL (caddy:2-alpine, port 80/443)
- traefik: Traefik reverse proxy with auto-SSL (traefik:v3.3, port 80/443/8080)

### Monitoring
- grafana: Grafana dashboards (grafana/grafana, port 3456)
- prometheus: Prometheus metrics (prom/prometheus, port 9090)
- uptime-kuma: Uptime Kuma monitoring (louislam/uptime-kuma, port 3001)

### Browser Automation
- browserless: Browserless Chrome (browserless/chrome, port 3100)
- lightpanda: LightPanda headless browser (lightpanda/lightpanda, port 9222)
- playwright-server: Playwright browser server (mcr.microsoft.com/playwright, port 3000)
- steel-browser: Steel Browser automation (steel-dev/steel-browser, port 3200)

### Search
- meilisearch: Meilisearch search engine (getmeili/meilisearch, port 7700)
- searxng: SearXNG meta-search engine (searxng/searxng, port 8080)

### Notifications
- gotify: Gotify push notifications (gotify/server, port 8083)
- ntfy: ntfy push notifications (binwiederhier/ntfy, port 8080)

### Communication
- matrix-synapse: Matrix Synapse homeserver (matrixdotorg/synapse, port 8008)
- mattermost: Mattermost team chat (mattermost/mattermost-team-edition, port 8065)
- rocketchat: Rocket.Chat communication (rocket.chat, port 3000)

### Desktop Environment
- desktop-environment: Desktop environment (various, port 6080/5900)

### Streaming & Relay
- livekit: LiveKit real-time communication (livekit/livekit-server, port 7880/7881)
- stream-gateway: Stream gateway relay (stream-gateway, port 8080)
- tailscale: Tailscale VPN (tailscale/tailscale, no exposed ports)

### La Suite Meet (multi-service)
- lasuite-meet-frontend: La Suite Meet frontend (port 8082)
- lasuite-meet-backend: La Suite Meet backend (port 8083)
- lasuite-meet-agents: La Suite Meet AI agents (port 8090)

---

## 4. Skill Packs (10)

| ID | Name | Required Services | Skills |
|----|------|-------------------|--------|
| video-creator | Video Creator | ffmpeg, remotion, minio | ffmpeg-process, remotion-render, minio-storage |
| research-agent | Research Agent | qdrant, searxng, browserless | qdrant-memory, searxng-search, browserless-browse |
| social-media | Social Media | ffmpeg, redis, minio | ffmpeg-process, redis-cache, minio-storage |
| dev-ops | DevOps | n8n, redis, uptime-kuma, grafana, prometheus | n8n-trigger, redis-cache |
| knowledge-base | Knowledge Base | qdrant, postgresql, meilisearch | qdrant-memory, meilisearch-search |
| local-ai | Local AI | ollama, whisper | ollama-local-llm, whisper-transcribe |
| content-creator | Content Creator | ffmpeg, remotion, minio, stable-diffusion | ffmpeg-process, remotion-render, minio-storage |
| ai-playground | AI Playground | ollama, open-webui, qdrant, litellm | ollama-local-llm, qdrant-memory |
| coding-team | Coding Team | claude-code, codex, redis, postgresql | redis-cache |
| knowledge-hub | Knowledge Hub | outline, qdrant, meilisearch, postgresql | qdrant-memory, meilisearch-search |

---

## 5. Presets (9)

| ID | Name | Services | Est. Memory |
|----|------|----------|-------------|
| minimal | Minimal | redis | ~1 GB |
| creator | Creator | ffmpeg, remotion, minio, redis | ~2 GB |
| researcher | Researcher | qdrant, searxng, browserless, redis | ~2.5 GB |
| devops | DevOps | n8n, postgresql, redis, uptime-kuma, grafana, prometheus | ~3 GB |
| full | Full Stack | All core services + all skill packs | ~8 GB |
| content-creator | Content Creator | ffmpeg, remotion, minio, redis, stable-diffusion | ~4 GB |
| ai-playground | AI Playground | ollama, open-webui, qdrant, litellm, redis | ~6 GB |
| coding-team | Coding Team | claude-code, codex, redis, postgresql | ~3 GB |
| lasuite-meet | La Suite Meet | lasuite-meet-frontend, lasuite-meet-backend, lasuite-meet-agents, redis, postgresql, livekit | ~4 GB |

---

## 6. Configuration Schema

### GenerationInput (POST /v1/generate body)

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| configVersion | number (int, >=1) | optional | Config version for migration support |
| projectName | string (1-64, kebab) | required | Project directory name |
| services | string[] | [] | Service IDs to include |
| skillPacks | string[] | [] | Skill pack IDs to include |
| proxy | "none" \| "caddy" \| "traefik" | "none" | Reverse proxy type |
| domain | string | optional | Domain for SSL (requires proxy) |
| gpu | boolean | false | Enable GPU support for AI services |
| platform | enum | "linux/amd64" | Target platform (linux/amd64, linux/arm64, etc.) |
| deployment | "local" \| "vps" \| "homelab" \| "clawexa" | "local" | Deployment target |
| deploymentType | "docker" \| "bare-metal" | "docker" | Docker or hybrid native+Docker |
| generateSecrets | boolean | true | Generate random secrets in .env |
| openclawVersion | string | "latest" | OpenClaw gateway version |
| monitoring | boolean | false | Include Grafana + Prometheus |

### ServiceDefinition Schema

Each service has: id, name, description, category, icon, image, imageTag, ports[], volumes[], environment[], healthcheck, command, dependsOn[], restartPolicy, networks[], labels, deploy, skills[], openclawEnvVars[], docsUrl, tags[], maturity, requires[], recommends[], conflictsWith[], minMemoryMB, gpuRequired.

---

## 7. CLI Reference

```bash
pnpm create better-openclaw@latest [directory] [options]

Options:
  --yes, -y              Use defaults (non-interactive)
  --preset <name>        Use a preset: minimal, creator, researcher, devops, full, content-creator, ai-playground, coding-team, lasuite-meet
  --services <ids>       Comma-separated service IDs
  --skills <packs>       Comma-separated skill pack IDs
  --proxy <type>         none, caddy, traefik
  --domain <domain>      Domain for auto-SSL
  --monitoring           Include monitoring (Grafana + Prometheus)
  --gpu                  Enable GPU for AI services
  --platform <arch>      linux/amd64, linux/arm64
  --deployment <target>  local, vps, homelab, clawexa
  --deployment-type <t>  docker, bare-metal
  --dry-run              Preview files without writing
  --open                 Open web UI in browser
  --output <dir>         Output directory (default: ./<projectName>)
```

---

## 8. Reverse Proxy Support

### Caddy
Auto-generated Caddyfile with:
- Per-service subdomain routing (e.g., `n8n.example.com`)
- Automatic HTTPS via Let's Encrypt
- Root domain routes to OpenClaw gateway

### Traefik
Auto-generated traefik.yml static config + per-service Docker labels:
- `traefik.http.routers.<service>.rule=Host(\`<service>.<domain>\`)`
- Automatic HTTPS via Let's Encrypt ACME
- HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect middleware
- Root domain routes to OpenClaw gateway (port 18789)
- Docker provider with `exposedByDefault: false`

---

## 9. Config Migrations

Configs include an optional `configVersion` field. The migration system automatically upgrades old configs:
- Version 1 → 2: Adds `deploymentType: "docker"` default
- Future migrations will be added as the schema evolves
- Configs without `configVersion` are treated as v1
